definition: ICT's foundational principle that price delivery is algorithmically timed — specific clock times correlate with specific types of price movement (accumulation, manipulation, distribution). Understanding TIME is as important as understanding PRICE. The algorithm delivers price in 20-minute macro cycles, and institutional activity clusters at predictable session times.
depends_on: market_philosophy (time-price is a core ICT belief: markets are algorithmic, not random)
feeds_into: sessions_and_kill_zones (kill zones are time-based trading windows)
silver_bullet (Silver Bullet is a time-specific entry model)
daily_bias (time of day confirms or invalidates the daily bias)

principle_1.name: Algorithmic Price Delivery
principle_1.explanation: Prices are delivered by algorithms, not by supply/demand in the retail sense. These algorithms operate on schedules tied to institutional settlement times, option expiry, and market open/close.
principle_1.implication: Price movements at specific times are NOT random — they are scheduled deliveries

principle_2.name: Time is More Important Than Price
principle_2.explanation: Knowing WHEN a move will happen is more valuable than knowing WHERE it will go. A perfect price level at the wrong time produces a losing trade.
principle_2.implication: Always check TIME context before entering — is this the right session, kill zone, and macro window?

principle_3.name: 20-Minute Macros
principle_3.explanation: The algorithm operates in 20-minute micro-cycles. Within each 20-min block, there is accumulation, manipulation, and distribution — a fractal PO3.
principle_3.implication: Entries and exits should be timed to the 20-minute mark boundaries

macro_timing.definition: 20-minute algorithmic delivery windows within each session
macro_timing.how_to_use: Identify which macro you're in. Don't enter during accumulation (choppy). Wait for manipulation (sweep) then enter on distribution (displacement + FVG).

time_of_day_expectations.midnight_to_0200_et: Asian session — range building, accumulation. Mark the high and low as liquidity targets.
time_of_day_expectations.0200_to_0500_et: London open — manipulation of Asian range, then trending move. Most volatile forex window.
time_of_day_expectations.0500_to_0830_et: London session — trend continuation. Decreasing volatility as NY approaches.
time_of_day_expectations.0830_to_0930_et: Pre-NY — news releases (NFP, CPI). High volatility, DO NOT TRADE unless experienced.
time_of_day_expectations.0930_to_1200_et: NY AM — highest liquidity. Best window for all instruments. Silver Bullet at 10:00-11:00.
time_of_day_expectations.1200_to_1330_et: Lunch hour — low liquidity, choppy price action. AVOID TRADING.
time_of_day_expectations.1330_to_1600_et: NY PM — second wind. Silver Bullet PM at 14:00-15:00. EOD positioning.
time_of_day_expectations.1600_to_1800_et: After-hours — low liquidity. Only crypto remains active.

day_of_week_expectations.monday: NWOG fill, range building. Often sets the week's high or low. Wait for direction confirmation.
day_of_week_expectations.tuesday: Trend day — often the strongest directional move of the week. Best day for entries.
day_of_week_expectations.wednesday: Midweek reversal. If Mon-Tue were bullish, Wednesday often prints the high then reverses.
day_of_week_expectations.thursday: Continuation of Wednesday's reversal or second leg. High-impact news common (ECB, etc).
day_of_week_expectations.friday: Profit-taking, reduced position sizing. Close intraday positions by 15:00 ET. Mark the close for NWOG.

bridge_integration.macro_window_detection: Track current UTC time and map to macro windows above
bridge_integration.avoid_trading_flag: Flag lunch hour (12:00-13:30 ET) and pre-news windows as no-trade zones
bridge_integration.day_of_week_modifier: Tuesday/Wednesday entries get +3 score bonus. Friday entries get -3 penalty.
bridge_integration.20_min_alignment: Prefer entries on 20-minute boundaries (:00, :20, :40) for algorithmic alignment
